# Faultsense

> Faultsense is a lightweight (6.5KB gzipped) browser agent that validates feature correctness in production through declarative HTML attribute assertions. Think E2E test assertions, but running against real user sessions.

- Docs: https://www.faultsense.org/docs
- Full reference: [llms-full.txt](./llms-full.txt)
- Examples: https://www.faultsense.org/examples
- GitHub: https://github.com/Fault-Sense/faultsense-agent

## How It Works

Add `fs-*` HTML attributes to the element a user interacts with. The attributes declare: what event triggers the assertion, what DOM or network outcome is expected, and how to verify it. The agent monitors real user sessions and reports pass/fail results to a collector.

## Attribute Reference

### Required Identifiers

- `fs-feature="<key>"` — Feature group (e.g., "checkout", "cart")
- `fs-assert="<key>"` — Unique assertion ID (e.g., "add-item", "submit-order")

### Trigger (exactly 1 required)

`fs-trigger="<value>"` where value is one of:
- `click`, `dblclick`, `change`, `blur`, `submit` — User interaction events
- `mount`, `unmount` — Element added/removed from DOM
- `load`, `error` — Resource loading (img, video, iframe)

### Assertion Types (at least 1 required)

DOM assertions — value is a CSS selector:
- `fs-assert-added="<selector>"` — Element matching selector is added to DOM
- `fs-assert-removed="<selector>"` — Element matching selector is removed from DOM
- `fs-assert-updated="<selector>"` — Element or its subtree is mutated (text, attributes, children)
- `fs-assert-visible="<selector>"` — Element exists and is visible (has layout dimensions)
- `fs-assert-hidden="<selector>"` — Element exists and is not visible
- `fs-assert-loaded="<selector>"` — Media element (img/video) finishes loading

Network assertions — value is the expected value:
- `fs-assert-response-status="<code>"` — HTTP response status matches (e.g., "200", "201")
- `fs-assert-response-headers='<json>'` — Response headers match (e.g., '{"content-type":"application/json"}')

### Modifiers (optional)

- `fs-assert-text-matches="<string|regex>"` — Text content of the asserted element matches
- `fs-assert-attrs-match='<json>'` — Attributes match (e.g., '{"src":"/img/logo.png"}')
- `fs-assert-classlist='<json>'` — Classes present/absent (e.g., '{"active":true,"hidden":false}')
- `fs-assert-timeout="<ms>"` — Override default timeout (default: 1000ms)
- `fs-assert-mpa="true"` — Persist assertion across page navigation (resolved on next page load)

### Network Association

Network assertions require linking a request/response to an assertion via the `fs-resp-for` header:
- Request header: `fetch(url, { headers: { "fs-resp-for": "<assertion-key>" } })`
- Response header: Server sets `fs-resp-for: <assertion-key>` on the response
- Query param: `fetch(url + "?fs-resp-for=<assertion-key>")`

## Placement Rule

Attributes go on the element that receives the user event. In event mode, only the exact `event.target` element is processed.

## Examples

### Button click updates DOM element

```html
<button
  fs-feature="counter"
  fs-assert="increment"
  fs-trigger="click"
  fs-assert-updated="#count"
  fs-assert-text-matches="Count: [1-9]\d*">
  Increment
</button>
<span id="count">Count: 0</span>
```

### Form submission with network validation

```html
<form
  fs-feature="contact"
  fs-assert="submit-form"
  fs-trigger="submit"
  fs-assert-response-status="201"
  fs-assert-timeout="2000">
  <input name="email" type="email" />
  <button type="submit">Send</button>
</form>
```

### Initial state validation on page load

```html
<div
  fs-feature="todo-app"
  fs-assert="initial-state"
  fs-trigger="mount"
  fs-assert-visible=".todos-empty">
  <!-- app content -->
</div>
```
